what_was_helpful: made me think about what to do next, and was nice to see all the 'parts' working
improvement_suggestions: thought i got better scores - question to reflcetion ratio etc

what_was_helpful: a
improvement_suggestions: b

what_was_helpful: I want the character selection to be in verbal form, not just wording and directive instructions. For example, now that you have chosen this character, please suggest ways to support them and options in a tick box.   Include open questions -  What challenges do you think Jan might face?  Additionally, consider including characters who have health or medical issues that could impact their performance. Please include real-life experiences.
improvement_suggestions: Unfortunately, I did not find the platform engaging and was unsure of what to expect or do next.   And felt that it should be more interactive, incorporating verbal recordings/ information about the characters, as well as polls or questions during the presentation, and photos/images.

what_was_helpful: The persona was very realistic and challenged me to practice active listening.
improvement_suggestions: Maybe add more difficult scenarios.

what_was_helpful: Didn’t find it very helpful at all.
improvement_suggestions:
Be clear about what the ‘reflection’ bit is doing. First time through (this is my 2nd attempt with this tool), I didn’t understand that it was using my input only (apparently not the conversation) to generate the ‘reflection’. 

As well, the advice was to ‘use more concrete examples’ in it’s response to me writing that it was challenging having the conversation without them. But that was the problem: the set-up didn’t give any concrete examples. That’s why I wrote that.

Also, both personalities (Mary & Terry) keep repeating the same phrases word-for-word. It makes it clear you’re chatting with a machine.

The overall experience is like playing a text adventure game where you’re trying to find the right phrase to answer the Sphinx’s riddle: you keep trying slightly different formulations to see which one works. There’s clearly no understanding or real dialogue happening.

what_was_helpful: Comments were clear that someone was not getting it - opportunity to go again from a slightly different angle.  If combined with vsual cues in real world this should in theory be even easier
improvement_suggestions: Actually the persona checked me when I hadn't closed the meeting properly (because I have things I need to get done today!) - a good reminder that you need to set aside enough time to do this properly!

what_was_helpful: terry
improvement_suggestions: nothing

what_was_helpful: It enabled me to think through before responding.
Terry does come across as a real high performer who is focussed on results rather than people.
improvement_suggestions: If I hear, "right, let's do this properly" one more time.....I'll leave it there. :)

what_was_helpful: being able to ask questions without fear of making a situation worse
improvement_suggestions: the responses intiially were repetetive and unhelpful, the constant use of the phrase "honestly" or "keeping the lights on" took away from the realism of the situation and prevented the conversation from moving forward. eventually i was able to get the bot to work towards a plan.

what_was_helpful: Seeing both how asking good, open questions encouraged reflection on collaborating more openly with colleagues. Also how someone with good technical knowledge and directness needs encouragement and tools to change their approach.
improvement_suggestions: It sometimes seems like the AI goes in a loop/repeats itself, but perhaps that's just a sign to take the conversation in a different direction. If you could build in some more detail/workplace examples they could speak about, that might avoid the loop of generic responses.

what_was_helpful: that the persona didn't shift until I demonstrated that I cared about the situation and its impact on them
improvement_suggestions: perhaps the ability to pause if you get stuck and ask for advice on possible ways forward

what_was_helpful: I'm not sure really, it didn't feel like a particularly natural conversation, I'm not sure a human would be that insistent in the way they speak, it didn't feel very real.
improvement_suggestions: I think humans would talk around a subject much more.

what_was_helpful: I suppose there’s some value in being able to consider responses to the AI’s provocations. But the conversation is squarely in the uncanny valley: one never really feels the investment one would have in a real conversation.
improvement_suggestions:
I really need to understand what y’all mean with ‘open questions’ and ‘complex reflections’. This is the third of three conversations, each time I received feedback that I didn’t use them enough.

As far as ‘open questions’, that’s nonsense. I’ve been conducting research interviews for 20 years. I know what an ‘open question’ is. The only ‘closed’ questions I asked where when I was seeking consent. 

I did quite a lot of reflection in this session. Whether it was ‘complex’ I’m unsure as I’m not familiar with that bit of jargon. 

Perhaps including definitions of these terms would help? Perhaps using a progressive reveal pattern, or maybe a glossary?

Also, the short answers from the AI are a bit irritating. A common research tactic — and one which is useful in these conversations as well — is being quiet. Letting the silence stretch a bit sometimes gives people space to open up. That’s not a move one can make here, and consistently getting short answers makes the ‘conversation’ really repetitive — same exact phrases over and over, whether they’re appropriate or not — in a way real ones often are not.

Yes, people do often repeat unhelpful phrases over and over. But not as mechanically or in appropriately as this. They usually work their way back around to them a bit. The AI just spits them back at you.

what_was_helpful: It was useful to be able to type things and re-read, pause and reflect, and alter things - it means the pace is a bit slower than in a real life conversation where you only get one chance.
improvement_suggestions: I think a clearer way of ending might be useful, otherwise it doesn't feel like you've got to the end and that you might need to keep going?

what_was_helpful: It made me think through and construct my answers carefully, based on the responses. Testing was helpful as a simulation.
The description of Terry at the start. It helped me think about how to approach the conversation.
improvement_suggestions: The feedback I got about the start of the conversation not creating a psychologically safe space and it being a bit uncomfortable for Terry contradicts the reflection that being playful build good rapport.

what_was_helpful: It felt very human and noted on specific points I was making
improvement_suggestions: Wasn't sure how to close out the conversation, I felt like the more I asked about anything else, the more the bot would presume I wanted to talk more, without the consideration of time, which would push the outcome of the question if the meeting time was coming to an end

what_was_helpful: practising some scenario based conversations
improvement_suggestions: its a little repetitive and maybe the scenario should include context like how long you have worked with the person.

what_was_helpful:
Implementing open questions with a varied audience.
It enabled me to practise the difficult conversations without worrying, or feeling judged! also enabled me to feel confident and the overview at the end was really useful.
improvement_suggestions:
i did have the same phrase back to me a few times by all of them which was slightly annoying, maybe more use of some other phrases. 
Also it felt like you was going on and on and on with questions, and it never ended.. perhaps it was me, but i did try to round it off a few times and it just came back with the 'i am just trying to keep my head above water' over and over again. 
also feel like the opening of the chat should be developed more, it felt straight away quite abrupt

what_was_helpful: I got to practice open ended questions
improvement_suggestions: Make it much more realistic, the bot keeps repeating itself. I haven't received any feedback on my responses, I just give you feedback & the session ends. I don't get anything back from the process?

what_was_helpful: Was interesting to have a conversation

It made me think, which was good.  
The repetitive elements made me consider the need for reinforcing and summarising
improvement_suggestions:
Mary fell off the chat and went silent on me!
All the chats were fairly limited - understandable at this stage, but if used on a larger scale a more enriched dialogue, perhaps with some additional work specific info might be helpful - rather than very high level and general

what_was_helpful: Sorry, very little - it obvious I'm talking to a machine & the conversation isn't realistic. Again the bot just keeps repeating itself.
improvement_suggestions: I don't think this is a viable mechanism - I know its not real & I'm talking to a bot.

what_was_helpful: I like the virtual play.  It was good to practice.
improvement_suggestions: When entering a persona, there is no context to what the conversation is about so you are just launched into a 'hello...'. It might be good to give a steer and say that the conversation is about talking to Terry about his attitude or improving his interactions with colleagues, rather than just be caught off guard.  It might also be a good idea to have attachments to be able to send you the transcript and the feedback to you as my feedback was to ask more open questions - despite every time I wrote back to Terry, I ended the paragraph with an 'open question'.  And where is the data based feedback to help with my coaching?  Surely it would be helpful if the feedback said 'You said to Terry xxxx, where you might try and say something like yyyy' so when I practice next time, I can try that approach and see if it works?

what_was_helpful: the feedback - helpful to see what I'd done well and where I could improve. I also really liked the reflection summary.
improvement_suggestions:
I think Mary's a bit too easy to please in that she wasn't resistant to any of my suggestions e.g .she could have said she was too busy to speak to Sarah  or to meet with me again.

In relation to the feedback, it would be good to get some examples of when in the conversation I could have asked open questions and what they could have been.

The feedback also said I could have done some 'complex reflection' with Sarah but I don't know what that means or how to do it.

what_was_helpful: It did feel good to practice in a safe space- I re-wrote a number of my questions before I actually pressed send so it was good to have the thinking time
improvement_suggestions: It would be good to have a bit more information about the work situation the person has or whether I can make assumptions about their role (e.g. I did assume they were a home worker and had a guess about them being able to come in to the office) and the AI will go with what I am assuming

what_was_helpful: This will come across harsh, but I genuinely did not find it at all useful.
improvement_suggestions:
The initial analysis claims I did not use ‘open’ questions. I did. 

The reflection said I did not mirror back. I did re-phrase and repeat, and received another repetitive reply.

what_was_helpful:
Very little, sorry!

The conversation wasn't realistic, Terry just kept repeating the same phrases over & over. There was clearly no memory of the previous point we discussed, each statement was like starting a new conversation.
improvement_suggestions: Terry needs to have better memory & not keep repeating the same thing as if we'd not discussed it 30 seconds ago.
Enable "flicking back" to the personas whilst in the session
